Bug / Task Description: RingBuffer.RemoveLastWhere corrupts the buffer when it removes an entry that is not the newest. After a matching entry in the middle or oldest position is removed, the compaction step that shifts the remaining (older) entries to fill the gap moves data in the wrong direction, leaving entries duplicated or out of order. As a result Entries() no longer returns the surviving entries correctly newest-first. Fix RemoveLastWhere so that after removing any matched entry, the remaining entries are intact and correctly ordered for both wrapped and non-wrapped buffers, with the evicted slot zeroed and count decremented.
Commit Subject: fix(ringbuffer): RemoveLastWhere must shift entries in the correct direction
